title: Rename pod on generate of container
body:
When generating kube of a container, the podname and container name in
the yaml are identical.  This offends rules in podman where pods and
containers cannot have the same name.  We now append _pod to the
podname to avoid that collision.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@fatherlinux fyi ... also we should consider a backport to v3.x.

Yeah, I'm not sure if it's worth back porting or not. I'll note for the Google searchers that find this PR/issue that you can work around this by manually creating a pod, then adding a container to it, then exporting the Kube YAML. If you just create a container, then export the Kube YAML, podman happily creates a Pod with the same name as the container which can cause this problem. It's an easy workaround, but not super intuitive to figure out how to work around.
